title: Ethereum Co-Founder Joseph Lubin's Dormant Wallet Moves $121.6M in ETH After 3+ Years
summary: Joseph Lubin, co-founder of Ethereum, transferred 80,001 ETH (worth ~$121.6M) from a wallet that had been inactive for over three years. The wallet still holds approximately 243,300 ETH (~$370M). The tokens have not yet been deposited into any known exchange.
body:
Joseph Lubin, a prominent co-founder of Ethereum, has moved 80,001 ETH (worth around $121.6M) from a wallet that had been dormant for over three years. This wallet still holds approximately 243,300 ETH, valued at around $370M. The transferred tokens have not yet been deposited into any known exchange.

The movement of such a significant amount of ETH from a long-dormant wallet has caught the attention of the crypto community. Lubin's wallet activity is particularly noteworthy due to his influential role in the Ethereum ecosystem. The exact destination of the transferred ETH remains unknown, adding to the intrigue surrounding this transaction.

For everyday crypto users, this news highlights the potential impact of large-scale movements by key figures in the industry. Such transactions can influence market sentiment and potentially affect the price of ETH. It also serves as a reminder of the importance of monitoring wallet activities of major stakeholders in the crypto space.

Moving forward, crypto enthusiasts and investors should keep an eye on the destination of these funds. If the ETH is moved to an exchange, it could signal potential selling pressure. Additionally, any further movements from Lubin's remaining balance could provide insights into his strategic intentions. Stay informed and watch for updates on this developing story.

The wallet still holds ~243,300 ETH (~$370M total). The transferred tokens have NOT hit an exchange yet. Where's it going? No one knows... yet.

Keep an eye on where these funds go—could impact ETH prices. Stay tuned for updates. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-06-ethereum-co-founder-moves-1216m-in-dormant-eth-after-3-years
